perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means all trade secrets, proprietary information, know-how, and confidential information of Company including but not limited to: (i) any and all technical, business or financial information or property, owned by or licensed to Company, or otherwise relating to Company and/or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates and related entities which is heretofore or hereinafter disclosed to Supplier, including but not limited to information regarding Company’s goods or services, processes, personne l, finances, business plans, studies, analyses, projections, research, market data, operations, apparatus, computer software, know-how, trade secrets, inventions, equipment, tools, molds, dies, fixtures, parts, prototypes, samples, drawings, test results, material and manufacturing specifications, suppliers, customers, employees, processes, licensing and any other ideas or information relating to Company’s business or Company Produc ts, the Supplies or any business or activity in which Company is engaged, regardless of the form of disclosure, whether or not disclosed in a writing marked “Confidential” or in some similar manner or identified as confidential;
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means all trade secrets and proprietary data of Company including but not limited to: (i) any and all technical, business or financial information or property, owned by or licensed to Company, or otherwise relating to Company and/or any of its subsidiaries, affiliates and related entities which is heretofore or hereinafter disclosed to Supplier, including but not limited to information regarding Company’s goods or services, processes, personne l, finances, business plans, studies, analyses, projections, research, market data, operations, apparatus, computer software, know-how, trade secrets, inventions, equipment, tools, molds, dies, fixtures, parts, prototypes, samples, drawings, test results, material and manufacturing specifications, suppliers, customers, employees, processes, licensing and any other ideas or information relating to Company’s business or Company Produc ts, the Supplies or any business or activity in which Company is engaged, regardless of the form of disclosure, whether or not disclosed in a writing marked “Confidential” or in some similar manner or identified as confidential;
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'confidential information' and 'know-how' from the definition of 'Confidential Information', the contract creates internal inconsistency. While the term is meant to be comprehensive, it now excludes core aspects of what would typically be considered confidential, leading to an in-text contradiction in understanding what 'Confidential Information' truly encompasses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Supplier shall use Confidential Information only to assist Supplier in making propos als to provide Supplies to Company and in providing Supplies to Company unde r Company’s purchase orders or other written agreements. Supplier shall not use or disclose Confidential Information except as required by the performance of its obligations to Company. Supplier shall disclose Confidential Information only to those employees of Supplier who (a) must have the specific Confidential Information either for the development, produc tion, testing and provision of Supplies or to decide whether to develop or supply the Supplies to Company; and (b) have signed this Agreement (or a similar agreement) binding such employees personally to the terms of this Agreement during and after their employment with Supplier. Supplier shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party, including Supplier’s related entities, without the prior written consent of Company and the written agreement of third parties binding them to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing 'confidential information' and 'know-how'. The contradicted text states that the Supplier shall use 'Confidential Information' only for specific purposes. By removing these terms from the definition, the changed text creates a contradiction because the Supplier's obligations regarding 'confidential information' and 'know-how' become unclear, weakening the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions within the document, and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information is acknow ledged by Supplier to be the property or an otherwise protectible interest of Company or its licensors whether or not it constitutes a trade secret.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information is acknowledged by Supplier to be the property of Company or its licensors only if it constitutes a trade secret.
perturbation.explanation: By restricting 'Confidential Information' to the status of property only when it is a 'trade secret,' there's a direct contradiction. The original text made all confidential information a protectable interest. This change now limits the scope and protectability which might create uncertainty and make the contractor violate the agreement if said information is not considered a 'trade secret'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition, Supplier shall take all reasonable precautions to protect the Confidential Information from unauthorized access and use, but not less than used by Supplier to protect its own most valuable information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all Confidential Information is a protectable interest. The changed text limits this protection to only when the Confidential Information is a trade secret. The contradicted text requires the Supplier to protect all Confidential Information, not just trade secrets. Therefore, the changed text contradicts the original agreement, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Supplier shall disclose Confidential Information only to those employees of Supplier who (a) must have the specific Confidential Information either for the development, produc tion, testing and provision of Supplies or to decide whether to develop or supply the Supplies to Company; and (b) have signed this Agreement (or a similar agreement) binding such employees personally to the terms of this Agreement during and after their employment with Supplier.
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier shall disclose Confidential Information only to those employees of Supplier who (a) must have the specific Confidential Information either for the development, produc tion, testing and provision of Supplies or to decide whether to develop or supply the Supplies to Company; and (b) have signed a confidentiality undertaking.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'this Agreement (or a similar agreement)' with 'a confidentiality undertaking' introduces ambiguity. It's unclear what constitutes a sufficient 'confidentiality undertaking,' making it hard to enforce. The original clause specifies the agreement's terms should bind employees, but now there is no specification on the terms of confidentiality, so it creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition, Supplier shall not and warrants that anyone obtain the Confidential Information disclosed hereunde r by or through it, shall not: (i) Contest, anywhere in the world at any time in the future, the validity of Company’s proprietary rights in, or any Company claim to the ownership or inventorship of, any Confidential Information; or (ii) Attempt to or actually apply for, obtain or claim ownership of, inventorship in or right to use of any proprietary rights in any Confidential Information (including, without limitation, any patents, copyrights, trademarks, service marks, trade dress, or trade secret rights).
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that employees must have signed 'this Agreement (or a similar agreement) binding such employees personally to the terms of this Agreement'. The changed text replaces this with 'a confidentiality undertaking'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text states Supplier warrants that anyone who obtains Confidential Information disclosed hereunder by or through it, shall not contest the validity of Company’s proprietary rights in, or any Company claim. Without a specified agreement binding them, it is unclear if a simple 'confidentiality undertaking' would sufficiently prevent them from contesting Company's rights, thus creating a contradiction and affecting the enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: If the law applicable to any enforcement proceeding limits the class of information and materials protectible by contract to trade secrets or other class of information, the term “Confidential Information” shall be limited to those classes of information and materials. If the law applicable to any enforcement proceeding requires a temporal limitation, the period after termination for which Supplier is restricted in use or disclosure of Confidential Information which is not a trade secret shall be limited to any specific maximum permitted by law or five years from the date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: If the law applicable to any enforcement proceeding limits the class of information and materials protectible by contract to trade secrets or other class of information, the term “Confidential Materials” shall be limited to those classes of information and materials. If the law applicable to any enforcement proceeding requires a temporal limitation, the period after termination for which Supplier is restricted in use or disclosure of Confidential Information which is not a trade secret shall be limited to any specific maximum permitted by law or five years from the date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Confidential Information” to “Confidential Materials” within the same paragraph creates confusion. The paragraph discusses limitations on protectable information, then abruptly switches terminology. This inconsistency implies these are separate concepts, when the intention is to refer to same thing, creating uncertainty. Since the begining of the document it defines what it's going to be used, as 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be construed unde r the laws of the State of Michigan, notwithstanding principles of Michigan or any other state, provided Company may in any enforcement action elect to have the law of the forum jurisdiction to apply. The parties irrevocably consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the jurisdiction in which Company has an office to resolve all issues related to or arising from this Agreement and any business relationship arising from or related to this Agreement. If the law applicable to any enforcement proceeding limits the class of information and materials protectible by contract to trade secrets or other class of information, the term “Confidential Materials” shall be limited to those classes of information and materials. If the law applicable to any enforcement proceeding requires a temporal limitation, the period after termination for which Supplier is restricted in use or disclosure of Confidential Information which is not a trade secret shall be limited to any specific maximum permitted by law or five years from the date of termination. If the law applicable to any enforcement proceeding requires a geographic limitation, the area in which Supplier is restricted in its use or disclosure of Confidential Information shall be limited to
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Materials" creates a contradiction because the paragraph subsequently refers back to "Confidential Information" without explicitly stating that "Confidential Materials" is synonymous. The contradicted text includes this changed text within a larger section, highlighting the inconsistency in terminology. This misalignment creates ambiguity and thus a strong contradiction as it is unclear which term is being referred to and if they are the same thing. As stated in the Perturbation Explanation, the document defined at the start 'Confidential Information', and this change goes against that.
